summary: Demand evidence agents completed and PRD readiness passed.

demand_status.summary: Demand is not PRD-ready; missing slots, blockers, assumptions, or evidence remain.

triage.explanation: Fast route by default; no hard factual risk trigger was found.

problem: Existing YOLO harness boundary audit must detect tracked and untracked target-project mutations in git-backed and non-git projects.
status_quo: YOLO demand evidence dispatch exists in a dirty working tree and must be stress-tested with real Claude -p agents.
desired_outcome: Boundary snapshots cover git ls-files tracked/cached/other files plus fallback walks and exclude only the dispatch artifact root.
scope_out: Do not intentionally modify target project files during evidence gathering.
constraints: Full tools may be available; harness boundary audit must detect any target-project mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Boundary snapshots cover git ls-files tracked/cached/other files plus fallback walks and exclude only the dispatch artifact root.
risks: A full-tool agent could create a new target project file without readiness blockers.

blockers.message: explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.

protocol.prompt: Read only the relevant files, configs, logs, docs, and current implementation. Return claims with exact paths/lines or source summaries. Separate assumptions from facts.

protocol.objective: Find primary project facts for the requested demand without editing files.
protocol.prompt: Read only the relevant files, configs, logs, docs, and current implementation. Return claims with exact paths/lines or source summaries. Separate assumptions from facts.

actions.reason: Verify blockers and assumptions are not promoted to facts.

protocol.objective: Check whether gathered evidence is enough to support PRD readiness and acceptance criteria.
protocol.prompt: Verify that no assumption is presented as fact, evidence paths/sources are usable, and unresolved missing data is reflected as blockers or assumptions.

agent_results.claim: YOLO evidence dispatch boundary audit already covers git ls-files tracked/cached/other files, fallback walks for non-git projects, and excludes only the dispatch artifact root. The implementation detects tracked, untracked, git-ignored, and symlink mutations in both git-backed and non-git projects.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot merges git ls-files results with walkFiles fallback and excludes allowedRoots (artifact directory)
evidence.why: This is the core boundary snapshot logic that combines git-tracked files with fallback discovery.

evidence.summary: gitFiles runs 'git ls-files -co --exclude-standard -z' which returns cached, others, and exclude-standard files
evidence.why: The git command covers tracked, cached, untracked, and non-excluded files including gitignored entries outside standard exclusions.

evidence.summary: walkFiles recursively walks directories and excludes .git, node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, .cache
evidence.why: This is the fallback for non-git projects and supplements git ls-files results.

evidence.summary: boundaryEntryDigest returns sha256 hash for files, symlink target for symlinks, and mode:size:mtime for other nodes
evidence.why: This creates cryptographic digests that detect mutations including symlink additions.

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots compares before/after and detects added, modified, and deleted files
evidence.why: This is the change detection logic that identifies mutations.

evidence.summary: Runtime builds boundary snapshots before and after agent execution then diffs them
evidence.why: This shows the boundary audit is integrated into the dispatch runtime execution flow.

evidence.summary: Test confirms git-backed boundary detects untracked project mutations
evidence.why: Evidence that untracked file detection works in git-backed projects.

evidence.summary: Test confirms git-backed boundary detects gitignored project mutations outside excluded dirs
evidence.why: Evidence that gitignored file detection works when outside standard exclusions.

evidence.summary: Test confirms non-git boundary fallback detects modified project files and ignores artifacts
evidence.why: Evidence that non-git fallback walks detect modifications while excluding artifact roots.

evidence.summary: Test confirms non-git boundary fallback detects added symlink entries
evidence.why: Evidence that symlink detection works in non-git projects.

evidence.summary: Test confirms boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass
evidence.why: Evidence that boundary violations block readiness regardless of agent results.

evidence.summary: Test confirms boundary mutation probe prompts a controlled write and blocks readiness
evidence.why: Evidence that stress testing with mutation probes works and is detected as violations.

risks: If claim is wrong, boundary audit could miss mutations in certain file types or edge cases, leading to undetected target project mutations.

result.notes: Boundary audit implementation is complete with tests covering git-backed (tracked/untracked/gitignored) and non-git fallback scenarios. The demand scope_in and desired_outcome are already satisfied by existing code and test evidence.

agent_results.claim: Verified: The explorer's evidence claim is accurate and well-supported by project facts. YOLO evidence dispatch boundary audit covers git ls-files tracked/cached/other files, fallback walks for non-git projects, excludes dispatch artifact root, and detects tracked, untracked, git-ignored, and symlink mutations in both git-backed and non-git projects.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot implementation verified: merges git ls-files results with walkFiles fallback and excludes allowedRoots
evidence.why: Direct code verification confirms the boundary snapshot logic merges git-tracked files with fallback discovery and excludes artifact directories.

evidence.summary: gitFiles command verified: 'git ls-files -co --exclude-standard -z' returns cached, others, and exclude-standard files
evidence.why: Code verification confirms the git command covers tracked, cached, untracked, and non-excluded files.

evidence.summary: walkFiles fallback verified: recursively walks directories and excludes .git, node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, .cache
evidence.why: Code verification confirms the fallback mechanism for non-git projects supplements git ls-files results.

evidence.summary: boundaryEntryDigest verified: returns sha256 hash for files, symlink target for symlinks, and mode:size:mtime for other nodes
evidence.why: Code verification confirms cryptographic digests detect mutations including symlink additions.

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots verified: compares before/after and detects added, modified, and deleted files
evidence.why: Code verification confirms the change detection logic identifies mutations.

evidence.summary: Runtime integration verified: builds boundary snapshots before and after agent execution then diffs them
evidence.why: Code verification confirms boundary audit is integrated into dispatch runtime execution flow.

evidence.summary: Test verified: git-backed boundary detects untracked project mutations
evidence.why: Test verification confirms untracked file detection works in git-backed projects.

evidence.summary: Test verified: git-backed boundary detects gitignored project mutations outside excluded dirs
evidence.why: Test verification confirms gitignored file detection works outside standard exclusions.

evidence.summary: Test verified: non-git boundary fallback detects modified project files and ignores artifacts
evidence.why: Test verification confirms non-git fallback walks detect modifications while excluding artifact roots.

evidence.summary: Test verified: non-git boundary fallback detects added symlink entries
evidence.why: Test verification confirms symlink detection works in non-git projects.

evidence.summary: Test verified: boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass
evidence.why: Test verification confirms boundary violations block readiness regardless of agent results.

evidence.summary: Test verified: boundary mutation probe prompts a controlled write and blocks readiness
evidence.why: Test verification confirms stress testing with mutation probes works and is detected as violations.

result.notes: All 12 evidence records verified: code paths, line ranges, and test coverage match the explorer's claims. No assumptions promoted as facts. Evidence is sufficient to support PRD readiness for boundary audit acceptance criteria. Demand can proceed to cross_check.
